name: Bizkaiko Bira: Burgoa Peak Loop from Bermeo
description: Hike the moderate 7.9-mile Bizkaiko Bira: Burgoa Peak Loop from Bermeo, offering panoramic views of the historic port and Bay of Biscay.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Bermeo are characterized by a diverse landscape within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO-protected area. The region features a dramatic coastline with impressive cliffs, notably around San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, and estuarine landscapes where the Oka River meets the Bay of Biscay. Inland, the foothills of Sollube Mountain offer more elevated perspectives and forested terrain. This combination provides a range of environments for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Bermeo The most popular hiking route is Tour of Biscay Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a segment of the longer regional trail, exploring diverse landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Saint John of Gaztelugatxe – San Juan de Gaztelugatxe loop from Gaztelugatxeko Doniene / San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, a moderate 1.7 miles (2.7 km) path. This route provides access to the iconic rocky islet and its hermitage, offering panoramic sea views. Local hikers also love the Bizkaiko Bira: Burgoa Peak Loop from Bermeo, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 51 minutes. This moderate loop provides views of the historic port and the Bay of Biscay. Hiking around Bermeo is defined by dramatic coastal cliffs, estuarine landscapes, and mountainous terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Bermeo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 4000 hikers have used komoot to explore Bermeo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Bermeo?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails around Bermeo, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 13 easy routes, 32 moderate routes, and 10 more challenging options, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Bermeo?
A: Yes, Bermeo offers several easy hiking trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31950015" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe – San Juan de Gaztelugatxe loop from Biotopo Protegido de Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.1 km path that allows you to experience the iconic coastal scenery without extreme difficulty. The region's diverse paths cater to various fitness levels, including those new to hiking.

Q: What are the best coastal hikes in the Bermeo area?
A: The Bermeo coastline is renowned for its dramatic cliffs and sea views. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10508313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe – San Juan de Gaztelugatxe loop&lt;/a&gt;, which provides access to the iconic rocky islet and its hermitage. Many trails within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve also offer stunning coastal vistas where the Oka River meets the Bay of Biscay.

Q: Are there any circular walks around Bermeo?
A: Yes, Bermeo features several circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327342913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bizkaiko Bira: Burgoa Peak Loop from Bermeo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.7 km circular trail offering views of the historic port and the Bay of Biscay. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327640814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sollube and Necrópolis de Tribisburu — circular desde el Truboimendi&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 11.95 km loop exploring mountainous landscapes and historical sites.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural monuments can I see along the hiking trails near Bermeo?
A: Hiking around Bermeo offers access to several notable landmarks and natural features. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, a rocky islet with a hermitage, or enjoy panoramic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269209" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views over the Urdaibai marsh&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning coastal views. The region is part of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO-protected area rich in diverse flora and fauna.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Bermeo?
A: The hiking trails in Bermeo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, from dramatic coastal cliffs and estuarine environments to the elevated perspectives offered by the foothills of Sollube Mountain. The well-maintained paths and the iconic scenery of places like San Juan de Gaztelugatxe are often highlighted as favorite aspects.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of Sollube Mountain?
A: Yes, the foothills of Sollube Mountain provide access to trails that offer elevated perspectives of the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327640814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sollube and Necrópolis de Tribisburu — circular desde el Truboimendi&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate circular hike that takes you through mountainous landscapes, offering views of Sollube and historical sites.

Q: What kind of natural environment can I expect when hiking in Bermeo?
A: Bermeo is situated within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO-protected area, offering a rich diversity of natural environments. You'll encounter dramatic coastlines with impressive cliffs, unique estuarine landscapes where the Oka River meets the Bay of Biscay, and forested terrain in the foothills of Sollube Mountain. This combination provides a varied and engaging experience for outdoor exploration.

Q: How long do hikes near Bermeo typically take?
A: Hiking durations vary depending on the trail's length and difficulty. For example, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327263506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of Biscay Trail&lt;/a&gt; segment is about 9.5 km and takes approximately 2 hours 45 minutes. Shorter, easy loops like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10508313" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in just over an hour, while longer moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327342913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bizkaiko Bira: Burgoa Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt; can take nearly 4 hours.

Q: Is Bermeo a good destination for birdwatching while hiking?
A: Absolutely. Bermeo is located within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, a UNESCO-protected area known for its rich diversity of flora and fauna. The unique estuarine landscape where the Oka River meets the Bay of Biscay creates a significant area for birdwatching, making many of the trails ideal for observing various bird species.

Q: Are there any historical sites to explore on hikes around Bermeo?
A: Yes, the region around Bermeo is rich in history. Beyond the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt; with its ancient hermitage, you can find trails that pass by old farmhouses and other historical sites. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1327640814" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sollube and Necrópolis de Tribisburu&lt;/a&gt; circular hike, for instance, offers insights into both mountainous landscapes and historical sites like the necropolis.

It's also next to the sculpture "Badatoz!" (here they come, in Basque), which is a monument to fishermen. The work depicts a family awaiting the arrival of their loved ones.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Burgoa Peak Loop from Bermeo?
faqs.answer: The Burgoa Peak Loop is categorized as a moderate hike. It requires good fitness and sure-footedness, covering 12.7 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 430 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain, including paved sections, unpaved tracks, natural paths, and asphalt. Hikers will encounter easily-accessible paths mixed with more natural surfaces.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable sights, including the View of the Old Port and the Fishermen's Monument, the Burgoa Summit, and the historic San Juan Gate. There are also panoramic views of the Bay of Biscay from Bermeo.

faqs.question: Where can I park, and how do I access the trailhead in Bermeo?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts and ends in Bermeo. As a vibrant town, Bermeo offers various parking options, though specific trailhead parking isn't detailed. It's advisable to look for public parking within Bermeo, especially near the old port, which is a common starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Bizkaiko Bira: Burgoa Peak Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, moderate hiking trails in the region are generally dog-friendly. It's recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife, and to always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this loop is generally during spring or autumn when the weather is milder and the scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be pleasant, but be prepared for warmer temperatures. Winter hikes are possible but may involve more challenging conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail or the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Burgoa Peak Loop or to access the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve. However, visitors are expected to respect the natural environment and follow general conservation guidelines.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially follows sections of the official 'Bizkaiko bira' trail (approximately 51% of the route) and also intersects with parts of '[CIMA BI05] Sollube * Bermeo' and 'Ardoaren eta arrainaren bidea'.

faqs.question: What amenities are available in Bermeo near the trail?
faqs.answer: Bermeo is a historic fishing village with a range of amenities. You'll find cafes, pubs, restaurants, and accommodation options, particularly around the old port area, making it convenient for pre- or post-hike refreshments.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect from the Burgoa Summit?
faqs.answer: From the Burgoa Summit, you can expect panoramic views of the surrounding Basque Country landscape, including the coastline, the Bay of Biscay, and the charming town of Bermeo below.

faqs.question: What is the significance of the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve for this hike?
faqs.answer: The trail is located within the Urdaibai Biosphere Reserve, an area recognized for its ecological importance. This means you'll experience a diverse landscape, including coastal features and green areas, offering opportunities to appreciate the region's natural beauty and biodiversity.
